div.h2: K Constant Derivation — Cube Root of Prime → 32-bit Hex

div.p: Security property: K[n] derivation is a one-way street. The designer cannot choose K values to embed a backdoor because each K is pinned to a universally verifiable mathematical constant (cube root of a prime). Any engineer can independently reproduce all 64 values: python import math primes = [2,3,5,7,11,13,17,19,23,...] # first 64 primes K = [int((p**(1/3) % 1) * 2**32) for p in primes]

Contrast: Dual_EC_DRBG used unexplained curve-point constants → NSA backdoor confirmed (Snowden 2013). SHA-256 is immune to this attack class precisely because derivation leaves zero degrees of freedom for the designer to aim at a backdoor-enabling value.
